title: Two dimensional ultrasonic array transducer with one dimensional patches
abstract: A diagnostic ultrasound system has a 2D array transducer which is operated with 1xN patches, patches which are only a single element wide. The "N" length of the patches extends in the elevation direction of a scanned 2D image plane, with the single element width extending in the lateral (azimuth) direction. Focusing is done along each patch in the elevation direction by a microbeamformer, and focusing in the lateral (azimuth) direction is done by the system beamformer. The minimal width of each patch in the azimuth direction enables the production of images highly resolved in the azimuthal plane of a 2D image, including the reception of highly resolved multilines for high frame rate imaging.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An ultrasonic diagnostic imaging system for display of 2D or 3D images comprising: an ultrasound probe having a two dimensional array transducer adapted to scan a target region with lxN patches of elements of the array, wherein the N dimension of the patches extends in one of the elevation or azimuth dimensions; a microbeamformer located in the ultrasound probe and coupled to elements of the array which applies delays to signals received from each patch to focus the patch signals in one of the dimensions; a system beamformer coupled to the ultrasound probe to receive the focused patch signals and apply delays to the patch signals to steer and/or focus the signals in the other of the elevation or azimuth dimensions ; a beamformer controller, coupled to the microbeamformer and the system beamformer, and responsive to a user control to control the scanning of a target region; an image processor responsive to the signals steered and/or focused by the system beamformer to produce image data; and a display, coupled to the image processor, to display a 2D or 3D image of the target region. 2. The ultrasonic diagnostic imaging system of Claim 1, wherein the microbeamformer further controls the timing of signals transmitted by each patch to be focused in the elevation dimension. 3. The ultrasonic diagnostic imaging system of Claim 2, wherein the microbeamformer further controls 

the timing of signals transmitted by each patch to be focused at a point focus in the elevation dimension. 4. The ultrasonic diagnostic imaging system of Claim 2, wherein the microbeamformer further controls the timing of signals transmitted by each patch to be focused as a plane wave in the elevation dimension. 5. The ultrasonic diagnostic imaging system of Claim 1, wherein the microbeamformer applies delays to signals received from each patch to focus the patch signals at a point in the elevation dimension. 6. The ultrasonic diagnostic imaging system of Claim 1, wherein the microbeamformer applies delays to signals received from each patch to focus the patch signals as a plane wave in the elevation dimension . 7. The ultrasonic diagnostic imaging system of Claim 1, wherein the microbeamformer further comprises a microbeamformer circuit which applies analog delays to signals received from each patch. 8. The ultrasonic diagnostic imaging system of Claim 7, wherein the microbeamformer focuses transmitted and/or received signals of the patches in the elevation dimension, and wherein the system beamformer further comprises a digital beamformer which digitally delays the elevation focused patch signals. 9. The ultrasonic diagnostic imaging system of Claim 1, wherein the two dimensional array transducer further comprises M rows of transducer elements with 

 - i y- each row comprising N transducer element, wherein M extends in the azimuth dimension and N extends in the elevation dimension. 10. The ultrasonic diagnostic imaging system of Claim 9, wherein the number of elements of each patch in the elevation dimension is the full N elements of each row of transducer elements. 11. The ultrasonic diagnostic imaging system of Claim 9, wherein the number of elements of each patch in the elevation dimension is less than the full N elements of each row of transducer elements. 12. The ultrasonic diagnostic imaging system of Claim 1, wherein the width of each lxN patch is equal to the width of a single transducer element. 13. The ultrasonic diagnostic imaging system of Claim 1, wherein the delays applied by the microbeamformer to signals received from each patch effect no focusing in the lateral (azimuth) dimension . 14. The ultrasonic diagnostic imaging system of Claim 1, wherein the wavefront produced by the microbeamformer delays is focused along a plane extending in the azimuth direction. 15. The ultrasonic diagnostic imaging system of Claim 14, wherein the wavefront produced by the system beamformer delays is focused along a plane extending in the elevation direction. The ultrasonic diagnostic imaging system of 

Claim 1, wherein the wavefront produced by the microbeamformer and system beamformer delays is steered and focused in both azimuth and elevation. 17. The ultrasonic diagnostic imaging system of Claim 1, wherein the two dimensional array transducer further comprises M rows of transducer elements with each row comprising N transducer element, wherein M extends in the elevation dimension and N extends in the azimuth dimension.
